# personal-memory-paths.txt — per-machine config consumed by install.sh
#
# WHAT THIS DOES
# ==============
# On each install.sh run, the installer reads this file and for each absolute
# directory path listed below, copies the current content of
# ~/.claude/rules/principles.md into a file named `principles_canonical.md`
# inside that directory. The synced file carries a header marker noting it is
# auto-generated and points at the canonical source.
#
# WHY
# ===
# Misha 2026-05-28: the operating rules live in TWO places — the harness
# canonical principles.md AND the personal-memory `feedback_dispatch_operating_rules.md`
# file. They could drift. Install-time copy keeps them in sync without
# replacing the personal-framed memory file (which stays as the user's
# editable, per-relationship narrative).
#
# FORMAT
# ======
# - One absolute path per line.
# - Lines starting with `#` are comments and skipped.
# - Blank lines are skipped.
# - Paths can contain spaces (NOT quoted — the entire line minus trailing
#   whitespace is treated as a single path).
# - $HOME and ~ expansion is supported.
#
# HOW TO USE
# ==========
# 1. Copy this file to ~/.claude/local/personal-memory-paths.txt
#    (the .example suffix becomes .txt; install.sh seeds it from this template
#    on first install if the target doesn't exist).
# 2. Replace the example paths below with the actual personal-memory directories
#    on this machine (they often live under
#    ~/AppData/Roaming/Claude/local-agent-mode-sessions/<agent-uuid>/<sub-uuid>/agent/memory/
#    on Windows, or similar agent-mode paths on macOS/Linux).
# 3. Run install.sh — the sync writes principles_canonical.md to each path.
#
# EXAMPLE PATHS (replace with your real paths; these will not work on your
# machine — they're shapes only):
#
# Windows agent-mode path:
# C:\Users\<your-username>\AppData\Roaming\Claude\local-agent-mode-sessions\<agent-uuid>\<sub-uuid>\agent\memory
#
# macOS / Linux agent-mode path:
# $HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/local-agent-mode-sessions/<agent-uuid>/<sub-uuid>/agent/memory
#
# After editing, lines that aren't comments / blank become active sync targets.
